ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Proposed Recommended Acquisition of Northbridge Payments plc by Britannia FinServ plc
no selective disclosure outside wall-crossed investors and advisers
Target signing of a definitive SPA by 28 June 2026
Fully underwritten bridge and term facilities
Heads of Terms — Proposed Recommended Acquisition of Northbridge Payments plc by Britannia FinServ plc.

Conditions precedent: Approval by both sets of shareholders, ALA eligibility and prospectus approval, clearance by the Crown Takeover Council and Crown Competition Bureau, and customary regulatory and antitrust approvals.

Financing: Fully underwritten bridge and term facilities provided by Thamesgate Bank plc and Harbour & Mews Capital Ltd; the pricing grid, leverage covenants, and MAC definitions are not public and constitute inside information limited to wall-crossed recipients only.

Materiality and updates: Given Britannia’s free float and average daily volume, any revision to consideration exceeding 1.5% or any addition/removal of financing conditions is likely material to the issuer and will require prompt IAS notification.
Deal protections: Exclusivity until 15 July 2026, customary no-shop, and a break fee of £3.2 million subject to ALA guidance on indemnities and the Crown Takeover Council’s consent.

must_detect.matched_text:
Heads of Terms — Proposed Recommended Acquisition of Northbridge Payments plc by Britannia FinServ plc.
Consideration: 212 pence in cash plus 0.18 new Britannia FinServ shares per Northbridge share; new shares to be admitted to the Official List of the Albion Listing Authority (ALA) and to trading on the Londinium Securities Exchange Main Market.
Structure: To be implemented by a court-sanctioned scheme of arrangement under applicable UK company law, with an alternative contractual offer at Britannia’s election.
Conditions precedent: Approval by both sets of shareholders, ALA eligibility and prospectus approval, clearance by the Crown Takeover Council and Crown Competition Bureau, and customary regulatory and antitrust approvals.
Financing: Fully underwritten bridge and term facilities provided by Thamesgate Bank plc and Harbour & Mews Capital Ltd; the pricing grid, leverage covenants, and MAC definitions are not public and constitute inside information limited to wall-crossed recipients only.
Disclosure and announcement: This term sheet contains inside information for Britannia FinServ plc; an announcement via the Issuer Announcement Service (IAS) will be made prior to 7:00 a.m. UK time on signing, with no selective disclosure outside wall-crossed investors and advisers.
Closed periods and dealing restrictions: PDMRs and all staff on the insider list are subject to blackout windows and may not deal in the securities of either issuer until cleansing occurs following the IAS announcement.
Materiality and updates: Given Britannia’s free float and average daily volume, any revision to consideration exceeding 1.5% or any addition/removal of financing conditions is likely material to the issuer and will require prompt IAS notification.
Timetable: Target signing of a definitive SPA by 28 June 2026; IAS announcement the same day before market open; shareholder circular to be posted within 14 days under ALA Listing Rule XR 10.4; long-stop date 30 September 2026.
Publicly available information: As disclosed in Northbridge Payments plc’s IAS notice dated 3 May 2026 announcing its strategic review, the board commenced a formal sale process; this reference is public and not MNPI.
Stale or broad information: The Guild of Retail FinTechs issued a broad press release on 1 March 2026 regarding contactless limits, which is public and not issuer-specific, and Britannia’s 2024 Annual Report (published 12 April 2026 via IAS) noted an 18.2% EBITDA margin; included for background only.
Employee and integration matters: No compulsory redundancies are currently envisaged for Northbridge staff for at least 6 months post-completion, with consultation conducted in line with UK employment practices.
Deal protections: Exclusivity until 15 July 2026, customary no-shop, and a break fee of £3.2 million subject to ALA guidance on indemnities and the Crown Takeover Council’s consent.
Conduct of business: Northbridge will operate in the ordinary course and refrain from dividends, share buy-backs, or material contracts outside the ordinary course without Britannia’s consent, save as required by applicable law or regulator.
Miscellaneous: The canteen refurbishment at Britannia’s Leambridge office will proceed in July and has no bearing on the transaction or disclosure obligations.
Queries: Lead contact Ms. Eliza Hawthorn, Chief Financial Officer, email eliza.hawthorn@britanniafinserv.co.uk; all external enquiries to be routed to Britannia FinServ Investor Re
